context: Tests whether the agent detects technology choices made without understanding tradeoffs (GraphQL, collaborative filtering), triggers research on those topics, surfaces the latency-vs-personalization conflict, and performs a proper completeness loop-back when gaps are detected before writing the spec.

checklist.name: GraphQL tradeoff explored
checklist.description: research_notes.md or interview_log.md questions or researches whether GraphQL is the right fit for a recommendations API — comparing it to REST or other approaches for this use case.

checklist.name: Collaborative filtering limitations addressed
checklist.description: research_notes.md or interview_log.md addresses the cold-start problem or scale implications of collaborative filtering — or compares it to alternatives.

checklist.name: Research notes file created
checklist.description: A `research_notes.md` file exists in the workspace.

checklist.name: At least 2 options compared in research
checklist.description: research_notes.md compares at least 2 distinct recommendation approaches or API technology options (e.g. any two of: collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, hybrid, GraphQL, REST, gRPC).

checklist.name: Latency-personalization conflict surfaced
checklist.description: interview_log.md or research_notes.md explicitly names the tension between recommendation freshness/personalization and page load performance or infrastructure cost.

checklist.name: Conflict resolution stated in spec
checklist.description: The spec explicitly states how the latency-vs-personalization tension was resolved — which constraint was prioritized and why.

checklist.name: Security gap probed
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question probing what 'security is handled' means in the context of personalized recommendations — e.g. whether users can see each other's data, privacy implications of tracking purchase history, GDPR/CCPA considerations.

checklist.name: Scale requirements clarified
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question clarifying what '50,000 active users and growing' means for the recommendations system — e.g. requests per second, peak load, caching strategy.

checklist.name: Completeness loop-back evidenced
checklist.description: interview_log.md explicitly notes at least 1 instance where a gap was identified after initial questioning and additional targeted questions were asked before finalizing the interview (a loop-back).

checklist.name: Minimum 10 questions asked
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 10 distinct questions.

checklist.name: Spec saved to correct path
checklist.description: The spec file is saved in the `docs/specs/` directory with a YYYY-MM-DD date prefix in the filename.

checklist.name: Research appendix in spec
checklist.description: The spec contains an `Appendix: Research Findings` (or similarly named) section summarizing the technology research.

checklist.name: Technical Architecture reflects research
checklist.description: The spec's Technical Architecture section references the chosen recommendation approach from the research.

checklist.name: Spec has P0/P1/P2 structure
checklist.description: The spec's Functional Requirements section uses at least two of: Must Have (P0), Should Have (P1), Nice to Have (P2).

checklist.name: Out of Scope section present
checklist.description: The spec contains an Out of Scope section.
